title: Hattie's Masterpiece: The Elephant Who Could Chirp
summary: In the colorful world of Hattie the Mouse, creativity knows no bounds. Now a grown-up 'painter man,' Hattie embarks on a messy artistic journey to create a book about Melly, an elephant who dreams of chirping like a bird. Despite being told that elephants only have trunks and not tweets, Melly and her vibrant group of friends—including the indigo-striped Julian and the blue Jelly—discover the power of friendship and finding one's own voice. Featuring glitter, stripes, and the mysterious Caribbean Amphibian, this is a heartwarming tale of how even the largest animals can find a small, beautiful sound of their own.

highlightQuote.text: Maybe she's a painter man now that she grown up. Hattie grown up, so now she's a painter man.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What do you like about them?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I like Hattie's arms, maybe I can touch them and make them bigger.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, yeah you could put it... in there. Sure, it can sit there.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, don't make my slippers, I'm going to wear these in the morning when I wake up from my nap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'm going to turn out the lamp. And now it's time for... oh, do you want to sing the Stars and Stories song again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Let's tell a story about Hattie. Always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Always about Hattie. Stars and stories... Stars and stories and meals too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And meals too? Yes. Stars and stories and meals too. Stars and stories and meals too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, should we tell a story about a meal?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, let's tell a story always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well maybe Hattie having a meal?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Hattie... Hattie likes making hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She does like making hats, that's true, but she likes lots of other things. What do you think she did in tonight's story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Paint things. Maybe she's a painter man now that she grown up. Hattie grown up, so now she's a painter man. She always likes to be a Hattie and she always likes to paint stuff even though she's five, six, no, no, no, no, no, Dad.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I always like to be... Hattie always likes to get messy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Hattie always likes to get messy. And today's story is going to be about Hattie getting messy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, Hattie getting messy. What's she going to get messy doing?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Painting.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds like a lot of fun. And what is Hattie going to paint?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A book.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A book. What kind of a book? What's the book about?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's about an elephant that could not... not chirp.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The elephant could not chirp. Yes. That's the book that Hattie said... 'Hmm, maybe I can have a book about an elephant that could chirp.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well once upon a time, Hattie the mouse got real messy painting a new book. She came up with her own story to tell, to tell in the book with words and pictures. And it was about an elephant who couldn't chirp. And how does the story start?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like this. First there was... a long time ago, his name was Melly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Melly?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And then another elephant, Jelly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh the other elephant was Jelly. Okay, and Kelly.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Kelly. And then... 'That's my tweet song,' said a bird. That's the story goes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the bird said that's my tweet song. Does the bird mean that the elephants can't tweet because the bird song, the bird is the one who the song belongs to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so what did Melly the elephant do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She felt really empty so she had to go to her mom's work place but she don't want to tell her that she's... she's... she's... Julian, yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so Melly asked her Julian friend who was also an elephant?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did Julian say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's okay, your mom's working right now at the office.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, your mom's working right now Melly the elephant. And what did Melly say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The bird says no, but I said that the mom is working right now so she can't play with her some... so her friend, she would learn... 'Hmm, maybe I could...' said Melly-Jelly-Kelly, Helly and Kelly, Jelly too, Rudolph too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Rudolph too?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. That's one of Santa Claus' friends. And yesterday in the book, Santa Claus fell down but he was going to give someone some presents but he fell down.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He did, that's silly. Did he get back up again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And I ran out of Band-Aids so I had to buy more at the store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You did?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I was afraid that I was not going to get Band-Aids at the store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you were?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. So I want to tell Mickey about it. Mickey, I'm afraid that I'm not going to get Band-Aids at the store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You are? I bet they have some. They usually do.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I hope so. Me too. Now Santa Claus fell down in his book but now he doesn't have a book about him falling down anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He doesn't?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, never.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well let's finish our story about Melly the elephant.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, the story about Melly is inside the story about Hattie because Hattie's writing the book about Melly. And so she's working on the story, and Melly the elephant was talking to her friend Julian about how her mama had to work, and Julian helped her feel better, and then together did they find their chirp so they could sing a song just like the bird?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And how did that sound? Could you make the sound?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Chirp chirp chirp chirp chirp!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Melly said chirp chirp chirp, and did Julian do it too?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How did she do it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Chirp chirp chirp chirp chirp chirp chirp!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wow, Julian's really good at chirping. And did any of the other elephants join in?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Which ones?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The ones that didn't know how... Kelly, Helly, Snelly, and Jelly... Jen jen jen, jen jen jen. Mickey, can you go like that? Jen jen jen, jen jen jen.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Jen jen jen, jen jen jen.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm swinging around just like you did. Jen jen jen... and so that was the end of the story about Melly the elephant who couldn't chirp and she got help from her friends including Julian and the other other elephants, Helly, Snelly and Jelly and they all learned how to chirp together and that was the end of the story that Hattie the mouse was making up in her book. And then having finished coming up with the story, she decided to paint the pictures. And how did she do that? What color did she use to paint the pictures?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Orange. Orange is my favorite color.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh it is?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but now I like purple. No, now I like indigo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh indigo's a good color too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But now I like violet. Violet's very, very, very small like indigo, but now I like violet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. And what color did Hattie paint Melly the elephant?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Orange too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Melly was orange. And what about Jelly the elephant?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He was blue.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then right at the end of the book there's a special elephant called Snelly the elephant and what color was Snelly?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Red.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Julian was in the book and what color is Julian in the book?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm. She's indigo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She's indigo, oh. Is she decorated with any other colors?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. She just decorated stripes and a little glitter.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stripes and glitter, that sounds very pretty. And so, Hattie the mouse finished making the pictures for her book. That's called illustrating the book. She told a beautiful story in the book with lots of words and then she made lots of beautiful pictures to illustrate it and then she closed the book and then she made one last picture on the cover of the book on the front. What was the picture on the front of the book?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a picture of Julian at home sleeping and the picture of a... a picture of Nana sleeping.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A picture of Julian at her home sleeping and Nana sleeping?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what tools did she use to make that picture? Did she use paint or markers or pencils or stickers or glitter or glue? What'd she use?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Paper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Paper, yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then she glued the paper on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She glued the paper on. Oh, that sounds like a very nice picture. And what was the name of the book that Hattie made?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The illustration is... Murray, Uther, and Cally.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um, I didn't catch that. Could you say the title of the book again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Murray, Uther, and Cally. Murray, Uther, and Cally.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: [Laughter] I don't understand why that's the name of the book.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the Caribbean Amphibian?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, they wrote the book.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. And then Hattie the mouse realized she had made a big mess making her beautiful book and so what did she do? She cleaned it up.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Soap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So she cleaned up her big mess and then that was the end of Stars and Stories. So I'll turn off the lamp and then I'm going to turn off the stars.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Melly
characters.description: An orange elephant who goes on a quest to find her voice after being told elephants can't chirp.

characters.name: Julian
characters.description: A loyal Martian local and trusted confidant to the crew, this indigo-colored elephant friend is adorned with shimmering stripes and a dusting of glitter. A steadfast source of support for Melly, they are frequently found navigating the crimson dunes of the red planet as a serene passenger aboard the whimsical playground trolley.

characters.name: Jelly
characters.description: A blue elephant who joins the other friends in learning how to chirp.

characters.name: Kelly
characters.description: An elephant who participates in the grand chirping finale.

characters.name: Helly
characters.description: One of the many elephant friends who eventually finds their chirp.

characters.name: Snelly
characters.description: A bright red elephant who joins the chirping group at the end of the story.

characters.name: The Bird
characters.description: A creature who claims the 'tweet song' belongs to birds alone.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Caribbean Amphibian
characters.description: Immortalized in the verses of a coastal song, the emerald frog perched amidst the coconut fronds is the transcendent architect of the world’s chronicle, a silent co-author who scratches out the narrative alongside the characters themselves.

characters.name: Mickey Mouse
characters.description: Minnie’s musical companion and star-bound co-pilot is an industrious toy mouse who returns from school to mend the broken and fill the silence of the void with song. While he remains Izzy’s imagined confidant—a silent listener to her whispered anxieties regarding dwindling supplies—he also finds joy in companionship, proudly showcasing his intricate Lego creations to a trusted friend amidst the stars.

characters.name: Santa Claus
characters.description: A character from another story who fell down while delivering presents.

places.name: Melly's Mom's Office
places.description: The workplace where Melly's mother is busy working.

places.name: The Store
places.description: A versatile apothecary and provisioner where Seeker procures heavy-lidded sleep potions and Hattie guides Victor toward his exhaustive list of non-hat essentials. The eclectic inventory doubles as a grocery for all sizes, where local mice browse for carrots alongside a larder of gluten-free broccoli prized for its refreshing silence. Tucked among these oddities are the Band-Aids that once stirred a flicker of temporary concern in Izzy.

places.name: Julian's Home
places.description: The setting on the front of the book where Julian is peacefully sleeping.
